courseTitle: GitOps for a Student Course Portal
headline: A pull request updates the desired state for a fictional student-facing learning portal
summary: Students act as release engineers for a course portal. They propose a content change, read automated feedback, and watch GitHub Pages reconcile the merged state into a live website.

changeRequest.title: Publish lesson 4 and tighten deployment feedback
changeRequest.description: Ship a new lesson page, surface faster CI feedback, and keep rollback simple for a teaching team supporting a student portal.

pipelineStages.name: Unit Tests
pipelineStages.purpose: Validate pure string and scoring functions before anything more expensive runs.

pipelineStages.name: Integration Tests
pipelineStages.purpose: Confirm that the built artifact contains the data and wiring the browser expects.

pipelineStages.name: Preview Review
pipelineStages.purpose: Review the rendered teaching site before merging the pull request.

pipelineStages.name: GitHub Pages Deploy
pipelineStages.purpose: Publish the merged desired state with fresh commit and deployment metadata.

learningPath: Open a feature branch and change the desired state in content/site-state.json.
Submit a pull request so CI can run fast upstream checks before review.
Use unit-test and integration-test feedback to decide whether the change is safe.
Merge into main after review so CD can rebuild the site from the merged commit.
Watch GitHub Pages publish the new page so live state matches the repository.
releaseNotes: The page now explains a release scenario instead of only showing raw deployment metadata.
CI separates upstream unit checks from downstream integration checks for clearer feedback.
Deployment metadata stays visible so students can connect Git commits to live output.
studentQuestions: Which parts of this release belong in Git-managed desired state and which belong in automation code?
What kind of mistake should fail fast in unit tests before the build even starts?
Why should integration tests verify the artifact the browser actually consumes?
